title: 02243_On_the_Power_of_Pre-training_for_Generalization_in_RL_Provable_Benefits_and_Hardness
positive_examples: Improves generalization in reinforcement learning (RL) which can enhance applications in autonomous driving, robotics, and healthcare, leading to better performance in dynamic environments.
Reduces the demand for real-world data by enabling agents to learn meaningful skills in simulated environments, thus improving test-time performance.
negative_examples: The theoretical results indicate that pre-training may not significantly improve performance in certain hard cases, which could lead to overconfidence in RL systems that are not adequately tested in real environments.

Without test-time interactions, achieving near-optimal performance in unseen environments is statistically intractable, which could result in deploying ineffective RL policies in critical applications.
